Home / Services / Wood Siding Installation

Wood Siding Installation

Classic wood siding installation, adding timeless beauty to your home.

For homeowners seeking a natural and elegant look, wood siding offers unmatched charm and character. Tema Roofing and Siding specializes in the installation of wood siding, carefully selecting high-quality materials that enhance your home's architectural style. We understand the unique challenges of wood siding in Philadelphia's climate, and we take extra precautions to ensure its longevity. Wood siding requires regular maintenance to combat moisture and rot, so we only use the best materials and techniques. We provide expert advice on wood selection, staining, and sealing to protect your investment. From traditional clapboard to modern shiplap, we have the expertise to bring your wood siding vision to life. We handle every aspect of the installation process, from preparing the surface to applying the final coat of sealant. Trust us to deliver a stunning wood siding installation that enhances your home's curb appeal and value. We focus on proper ventilation and drainage to minimize moisture damage and extend the life of your wood siding.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Wood Selection – Choosing the right wood species and style for your home.
  2. 02 Step 2: Preparation – Priming and sealing the wood to protect against moisture.
  3. 03 Step 3: Precise Installation – Careful fitting and fastening of wood siding planks.
  4. 04 Step 4: Finishing – Applying stain or paint for a beautiful and durable finish.

Benefits

  • Natural beauty
  • Elegant aesthetic
  • Increased home value
  • Customizable look

Frequently Asked Questions

What type of wood is best for siding in Philadelphia?

Cedar and redwood are excellent choices for wood siding in Philadelphia due to their natural resistance to moisture and decay.

How often should wood siding be stained or sealed?

Wood siding should be stained or sealed every 3-5 years to protect it from the elements and prevent rot.

What are the signs that my wood siding needs repair?

Signs of wood siding damage include cracks, rot, peeling paint, and insect infestations.
